title: My life as a Russian exile in Paris: 'You can't fight French bureaucracy'
subhead: 'A Russian in Paris' (1/6). Journalist Elizaveta Osetinskaya, who came to France in 2022 because of her opposition to the war in Ukraine, describes the difficulties she has faced with the prefecture while barely speaking the language.

title: United States announces civilian nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
US House passes record $1.15 trillion defense bill
Wildfires ravage parts of southern France, Italy and Spain
subhead: Thousands have been evacuated amid wildfires that have been raging for days across several regions in Europe. Scientists agree that climate change caused by humans burning fossil fuels is making extreme weather events such as fires increasingly severe.

title: After exoplanets, the hunt for 'exomoons' begins
subhead: An international team has identified an object orbiting a brown dwarf, which itself revolves around a star twice as small as our Sun. The findings were published Wednesday in the journal 'Nature.'

title: Looking back on Balenciaga's apocalyptic runway show in March 2020
subhead: 'Behind the scenes' (1/6). Runway shows are pivotal moments for fashion houses, often providing an opportunity for particularly spectacular productions. In March 2020, creative director Demna devised a hellish presentation for Balenciaga.

title: Friterie des Nations, a local institution at the Calais Beach
subhead: 'Gourmet kiosks' (1/6). Across France, small kiosks serving local specialties are a favorite stop for holidaymakers. In Calais, northern France, Pascal Carré and his daughter Loranne serve fries that are crisp on the outside and soft on the inside.

How social media bans for young users, now in effect in France, have spread worldwide
subhead: France has decided to ban those under 15 from major platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. The country is following a global trend that has gained momentum in recent years.

title: New monkey species identified in Lomami National Park, Democratic Republic of Congo
subhead: First photographed in 2008, this small colobus monkey with pink-orange lips has now been officially described by an international team of researchers – and may be immediately classified as 'endangered.'

title: Climate scientists aren't surprised by summer's record heat: 'We've entered a dangerous zone'
subhead: Record heat, historic drought and sweeping wildfires have marked France's 2026 summer. Yet climate scientists mostly see global warming's expected effects playing out. Only the rapid succession of heatwaves has caught them off guard.

title: France's agriculture law puts government in a bind
'Stopping AI because it carries risks? That would be like banning cars because they cause accidents'
'We are trapped in a climate dystopia, a black comedy scenario, where the grotesque rivals the Orwellian farce'
